ads.title: Faculty — Applied Mechanics and Biomedical Engineering
ads.raw_text_excerpt: (a) At least one pre-PhD engineering degree (at the bachelor's or master's level) Specialization Area* (b) Post-doc research experience preferred * The department specializes in interdisciplinary research that broadly spans the fields of Solid Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, and Biomedical Engineering. Applications are invited from candidates with strong engineering acumen and an interdisciplinary focus in addressing contemporary critical challenges that fits into at least one of the following specializations: (i) Computational Solid Mechanics focussing on cutting-edge methodologies that bridge solid mechanics and scientific computing, addressing fundamental challenges in areas such as multiscale/multiphysics modelling, phase field and nonlocal approaches to failure, advanced discretization methods, and the integration of physics-informed machine learning with rigorous mechanics-based modelling. (ii) Topology Optimization integrating active material mechanics for multifunctional adaptive systems. (iii) Experimental thermal–fluid science with a preference for research relevant to clean energy systems, advanced cooling technologies, micro-gravity environments or micro & modular nuclear reactors. (iv) AI-driven algorithm development for fluid mechanics. 1 Annexure -1(ver.2, dt.16.04.2026) (This version of Annexure‐1 supersedes all previous versions of Annexure‐1 released during this advertisement period. Applicants shall always ensure that they are referring to the current version of this Annexure ‐1, posted on https://facapp.iitm.ac.in/2026ra /). (v) Medical Instrumentation and Biomedical Signal/Image Processing: Novel clinical measurement and imaging systems (electrophysiology, clinical imaging modalities, surgical and interventional instruments) and the processing of physiological recordings (EEG, EMG, ECG, evoked potentials) and in vivo medical images for diagnosis, monitoring, surgical planning, and rehabilitation tracking. (vi) Physiological Systems Modelling: Computational and data-driven models of organ-system physiology (cardiovascular, musculoskeletal, respiratory) useful for patient-level prediction or control. ads.title: Faculty — Aerospace Engineering
ads.raw_text_excerpt: Established in 1964, the aerospace engineering department is among the leading departments for research and education in aerospace engineering comprising the core specializations of aerodynamics, flight mechanics, propulsion, and aerospace structures. Over the years, the department has engaged with a large number of organizations including NAL, GTRE, HAL, DRDL, ADE, ARDE, ADA, ADRDE, ISRO, IAF, USAF, BARC, Boeing, Pratt and Whitney, Airbus and GE for scientific and engineering research. The department seeks applications from dynamic and motivated candidates with excellent academic record along with specialization in the any of the following domains: Aerodynamics stream : – Aeroacoustics; Aerodynamics of bio-inspired vehicles; Fluid-structure interactions (FSI), computational FSI; High-fidelity computational aerodynamics simulation for low/high-speed flow regimes; Testing and instrumentation for low/high-speed flows. Aerospace propulsion stream : – Turbomachinery: experimental methods, full-scale computations, and flutter; Electric propulsion; Novel propulsion systems for drones and space applications; Solid propellants. Aerospace structures stream : – Dynamics and vibrations of flexible structures; Aeroelasticity: experimental and computational methods; Experimental solid mechanics; Design of aerospace vehicle structures (including vehicles for space exploration); Structures for spacecraft and satellite applications; Structural integrity and health monitoring. Flight dynamics and controls stream : – Flight dynamics of aircraft (fixed, rotary and flapping wings), coupled computational aeromechanics and control; Satellite and spacecraft dynamics; Flight vehicle guidance and control. Other areas : – Space vehicles and payload technologies; Green/Sustainable aviation; Stealth technologies for aerospace applications; Scientific machine learning surrogates and digital twins for aerospace applications; Unmanned aerial vehicle technology (design, integration, guidance & control, and their applications); Multi-disciplinary design optimization (MDO); Designs for futuristic flight vehicles; Avionics and aerospace systems, Integrated vehicle health management; Coupled aero-structure-EM computations; Internal aerodynamics; Novel sensors for aerospace and space applications; Next-generation materials and manufacturing processes for aerospace applications; High energy material/energetics for aerospace applications; It is strongly desirable that at least one of the degrees should be in Aerospace/Aeronautical engineering. The department website link is https://www.iitk.ac.in/aero Professor Sanjay Kumar, HOD This email address is being protected from spambots. You need JavaScript enabled to view it.

ads.title: Faculty — Biological Sciences and Bioengineering
ads.raw_text_excerpt: Biological Sciences and Bioengineering (BSBE) is a multi-disciplinary department addressing both basic and applied areas of Life Sciences and Bioengineering. Research in BSBE is focused on: regenerative medicine (stem cell biology, tissue engineering, drug delivery, biomedical device); understanding protein structure-function relationship (structural and computational biology); engineering approaches to understanding biological systems (biomechanics, image processing, electrophysiology, computational modeling), cellular, molecular, developmental, and neuro-biology addressing fundamental aspects of cell differentiation and growth; disease models - animal models for human diseases. Applications for faculty positions at all levels are invited in the areas. Biomedical Engineering – Electrical/Electronics/Mechanical engineers developing biomedical sensors, devices and diagnostics are particularly encouraged to apply, The other areas of interest are - Neuroengineering, Biomaterials, Tissue eengineering and drug delivery systems. Structural and computational biology - Computational biology with specialization in AI/machine learning, big data and all related areas, biomolecular simulations, systems biology, synthetic biology, Microbiology and immunology - Infectious disease and antimicrobial resistance, Immunology, Cancer Immuno-Therapy, Immune engineering. Basic Sciences – Cell Biology, Developmental Biology using classical and molecular genetic approaches in model organisms Applicants are expected to organize and participate in multi-disciplinary research and contribute to teaching both at the undergraduate and graduate levels. Further, applicants with exceptional achievements in any area of Biological Sciences and Bioengineering are also welcome to apply. The 5 Centers that DIS faculty will interact with are: Wadhwani Center for Advanced AI (CAA) Wadhwani Center for Developing Intelligent Systems (CDIS) Wadhwani Center for Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity of Cyber-physical Systems (C3i) Wadhwani Center for Robotics (CR) Wadhwani Center for AI Policy and Outreach (CAPO). Research Areas of Interest We welcome applications in a broad range of research areas, including but not limited to: Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning Data Science and Big Data Analytics Natural Language Processing Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Embedded and Cyber-Physical Systems Optimization and Control Robotics and Autonomous Systems Pervasive and Mobile Computing

ads.pdf_excerpt: Applications are invited to hire Energy Manager, Energy Assessment Engineer and Field technicians for Kotak-IITM Save Energy Mission (KISEM) at IIT Hyderabad. The primary objective of this project is to conduct an energy assessment for MSMEs to enhance their energy efficiency and reduce their overall carbon footprints. The KISEM project team at IIT Hyderabad comprises eight Professors from various domains, a Certified Energy Auditor, Energy Manager and Energy Assessment Engineers. The selected candidates will be involved in carrying out walk-through and detailed energy assessments for specific process industries that come under the MSME category and come up with various customized energy conservation measures in the form of the assessment report and guide the industries in all possible ways to implement the same.

ads.raw_text_excerpt: Ashoka University, Department of Sociology & Anthropology — Visiting Faculty Positions (Open Rank | 2026–2027). Sonipat, Haryana. Open: 1 May 2026. Deadline: 1 Jun 2026 (11:59 PM ET). Semesters: Monsoon (25 Aug – 13 Dec 2026), Spring (19 Jan – 9 May 2027). Apply for one or both semesters. Preferred areas: STS, Gender & Sexuality, Caste, Legal Anthropology, Medical Anthropology. Qualifications: PhD; strong teaching; active research. Responsibilities: Teach 2 courses/semester; engage in academic activities. Apply with: Cover Letter, CV, Syllabi, Teaching Statement.
